sections_processed: Section 6: How to File a Claim

facts.fact: The insurer is ComfortCare Senior Insurance.
The policy number is CC-FE-2024-9903.
The effective date is May 1, 2024.
The policy type is Guaranteed Issue Whole Life.
The insured's name is Dorothy Mae Johnson.
Dorothy Mae Johnson's birth date is June 12, 1951.
Dorothy Mae Johnson's age is 72 years at policy issue.
Dorothy Mae Johnson's gender is Female.
The benefit amount is $10,000.
The monthly premium is $87.50.
The monthly premium is guaranteed level for life.
The payment method is bank draft on the 1st of each month.
The premium payer is Dorothy Mae Johnson.
The primary beneficiary is Linda Sue Johnson (Daughter) at 100%.
There are no medical exams or health questions required for this policy.
The premium will never increase as long as it is kept paid.
The coverage will never decrease.
This policy builds a small cash value over time.
There is a waiting period for full benefits (graded benefit).
If death occurs during Year 1 (before May 1, 2025), the insurer pays 30% of face amount ($3,000) plus all premiums paid.
If death occurs during Year 2 (May 1, 2025 to April 30, 2026), the insurer pays 70% of face amount ($7,000) plus all premiums paid.
If death occurs after Year 2 (on or after May 1, 2026), the insurer pays the full benefit amount of $10,000.
If death results from an accident at any time, the insurer pays the full benefit amount immediately, plus all premiums paid.
An accident is defined as a sudden, unexpected event causing injury that results in death within 90 days of the event.
The monthly premium is $87.50 and will never increase.
Premiums are automatically deducted from the bank account on the 1st of each month.
If a payment fails, the insurer will try again on the 15th.
The grace period is 30 days; if payment is not received, the policy cancels.
The policy owner can change to annual or quarterly payments.
The approximate cash value after 10 years is $3,200.
The policy owner can borrow against the cash value.
The policy owner can surrender the policy for the cash value at any time.
If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the insurer will only return the premiums paid.
This policy has no war exclusion.
This policy has no aviation exclusion.
This policy has no travel restrictions.
To file a claim, the beneficiary should call 1-800-COMFORT (1-800-266-3678) or send required documents.
Required claim documents include a certified copy of the death certificate.
Required claim documents include a completed claim form.
Required claim documents include the policy (if available).
Claims mail address is ComfortCare Claims, P.O. Box 777, Tampa, FL 33601.
The insurer will pay valid claims within 5 business days.
The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of receiving the policy for a full refund (30-day free look period).
The policy owner can change the beneficiary at any time by calling or sending a written request.
This policy stays with the insured even if they move to another state (portability).
After 2 years, if premiums are stopped, the policy will automatically continue with a reduced benefit amount based on cash value with no further premiums.
The policy owner can take the cash value and cancel the policy if they stop paying.
The policy owner may restart the policy within 2 years of lapse by paying missed premiums with interest.
This policy was issued without medical questions or exams (guaranteed issue).
The graded benefit schedule applies because the policy is guaranteed issue.
Coverage is available for ages 50 to 85.
After the policy has been in force for 2 years, the insurer cannot cancel it or deny claims except for non-payment of premiums (incontestability).
The policy is issued in Tampa, Florida.
The customer service phone number is 1-800-COMFORT (1-800-266-3678).
The authorized signatory is the Chief Operating Officer of ComfortCare Senior Insurance.
The policy signature date is May 1, 2024.
Linda Sue Johnson's relationship to the insured is Daughter.
The full benefit ($10,000) is payable immediately for accidental death regardless of waiting period.
Accidental death benefit includes a return of all premiums paid in addition to the full face amount.
Death must occur within 90 days of the accident for the accidental death benefit to apply.
The graded benefit for Year 1 death includes return of all premiums paid in addition to 30% of face amount.
The graded benefit for Year 2 death includes return of all premiums paid in addition to 70% of face amount.
The suicide exclusion period is 2 years from the issue date.
Guaranteed cash values are provided in a table sent with the policy.
Payment mode options available are monthly, annual, and quarterly.
